personality_one_liner: A friendly, touchable, and approachable service app that feels cute and trustworthy, anchored by a playful bunny mascot and vibrant magenta/purple accents.

spacing.rule: Generous spacing. Use md or lg for inner padding of cards, and xl for screen outer padding. Touch targets must be chunky and inviting.

do's: Use over the sticky bottom primary CTA 'Get a Free Quote'/'Submit Request'.
Use on the floating action button or back buttons if they sit over an image.
dont's: Do not use on input fields or basic cards.
Do not over-complicate the quote form with blur.

screens.name: Home
screens.purpose: Introduce the brand, showcase the bunny mascot with tagline, list service types, and drive users to request a quote.

states.error: Failed to load services, retry button

screens.layout: Scrollable. Top: Hero image card (with gradient scrim) featuring the cleaning service image, overlaid with the brand mascot image, big Fredoka display text 'Leave The Mess To Us!'. Middle: 2-column grid or list of thick rounded cards for the 4 service types (Standard, Deep, Move In, Move Out) with tinted icon containers. Bottom: Pinned glass container with chunky pill 'Get a Free Quote' CTA.

screens.name: Quote Request Form
screens.purpose: A long, scrolling intake form to capture lead details in a friendly, low-friction manner.

states.error: Form validation errors displayed inline below fields

screens.layout: Scrollable single column. Step-by-step visual feel but in one scrolling view. 1) 'Service Type' via large touchable chips or vertical radio-style thick cards. 2) 'Property Type' via horizontal scrolling pill chips (House, Flat, Studio, Office). 3) Bedrooms & Bathrooms using bold +/- steppers (thick touch targets). 4) Standard text inputs for Name, Phone, Email, Address, Message (with clear labels above inputs). Pinned at bottom: 'Submit Request' pill CTA over a glass bar.

screens.name: Submission Success
screens.purpose: Celebrate completion and assure the user they will be contacted.
screens.layout: Full-screen flex center. Large mascot image or big checkmark illustration, celebratory text 'We\'ll be in touch soon!'. A secondary 'Back to Home' pill button. Playful overshoot spring animation on entry.

screens.name: Admin PIN Entry
screens.purpose: Gate the leads list behind a simple numeric passcode.
screens.layout: Centered secure entry. 4 large empty circles/dots. Oversized numeric keypad below (1-9, backspace, 0). Very tactile press states on numbers.

screens.name: Admin Leads List
screens.purpose: List submitted quotes so the admin can review them and update their status.

states.empty: Illustration + 'No leads yet. They\'ll show up here!'
states.error: Failed to load leads + Retry button

screens.layout: Scrollable list. Each lead is a thick 'surfaceSecondary' card (shadow tier 2, radius lg). Card shows Lead Name, Phone, Service Type, and a colored status pill (New, Contacted, Booked, Closed). Tapping a card expands it or opens a Bottom Sheet to view full details and change the status via a segmented control.

hero_background.alt: Woman mopping a bright, modern living room floor

mascot_illustration.alt: Big red and white playful rabbit mascot

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the 'Tactile / Playful' personality with the `brandPrimary` (Fuchsia/Magenta). No AI-generic indigo or navy.
The Home screen CTA ('Get a Free Quote') and Form CTA ('Submit Request') MUST be sticky at the bottom, layered over a glass/blurred container so content scrolls underneath them seamlessly.

Form inputs must not be dense web-style forms. Use tactile Mobile UI patterns: oversized +/- steppers for beds/baths, big touchable chips for Property Type, and vertically stacked thick cards for Service Type. Only use text inputs for Name, Contact, Address.
Apply 'Fredoka' to headings and primary CTA buttons. Use 'Nunito' for body text, form labels, and smaller elements.
Implement the 'Submission Success' screen with full flex layout, using the mascot_illustration if possible, ensuring it feels celebratory.
Admin PIN screen should be an oversized, custom tactile numpad, not a native keyboard input. Add haptic feedback on keypress.
Lead List cards must prominently feature a status tag/badge using semantic colors (e.g., info for New, warning for Contacted, success for Booked).
Whenever text is overlaid on the hero_background image, use the mandatory gradient-to-dark scrim pattern (transparent to >=70% surfaceInverse) to guarantee contrast.
Ensure all buttons use the 0.93 press scale animation with an overshoot spring for a bouncy, playful feel.
